category_title: Students with intellectual disabilities who develop confidence through retail customer service
minimal_context: Eric is sitting in the special education classroom during pre-placement orientation, tasked with completing paperwork to prepare for a workplace safety test.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Eric, please sit down and fill out this paperwork to prepare for your workplace safety test.

STUDENT: I want to be a doer and work with my hands.

TUTOR: Just do your best to answer the questions on the page.

STUDENT: But here we, you know, sit down and just do paperwork. I want to learn skills that you develop along the way that you won't learn in school.

TUTOR: You need to know this information for the test.

STUDENT: I'm just going to learn this for your test and then forget about it. I need hands-on learning.

options.A: Find ways to help the student make connections between the workplace and the current paperwork.
options.B: Ignore the student completely due to the undesirable behavior during the current interaction.
options.C: Maintain close physical proximity to the student (within 0.3 meters) during the interaction.
options.D: Immediately remove all demands and paperwork materials for 60 seconds during the session.
